the code is straightforward.\n- No signs of backdoors, secret keys, or malicious payloads are present.\n- The code's purpose is purely interface creation, with no external data processing.\n\n**Conclusion:**\n- The scores assigned in the reports are consistent with the code's nature.\n- Given the code's simplicity and standard pattern, I agree with the low malware and obfuscated scores.\n- The security risk score could remain at 0.1, reflecting minimal inherent risk but acknowledging that interacting with blockchain contracts always carries some external risk.\n\n**My overall estimate:**\n- **Malware score:** 0 (no malicious behavior detected)\n- **Obfuscated score:** 0 (clear, standard autogenerated code)\n- **Risk score:** 0.1 (very low, typical for utility code but interaction with blockchain always involves external risks)\n\n**Final note:** This code is benign, standard boilerplate, and poses no security threat in its current form.",
